units.description: Canonicality precondition: the denomination system must be canonical (greedy yields the optimal count). (Variant 5 stresses monotonicity or stability properties implied by the reference implementation.)
common_failure_modes: assuming greedy is always optimal for any denomination system

units.description: Alignment: the output counts vector has the same length as denoms. (Variant 5 stresses monotonicity or stability properties implied by the reference implementation.)
Decomposition: sum(counts[i] * denoms[i]) equals amount. (Variant 5 stresses monotonicity or stability properties implied by the reference implementation.)
common_failure_modes: stating sum(counts) = amount instead of the weighted sum

units.description: Optimality: sum(counts) is minimal among all valid decompositions. (Variant 5 stresses monotonicity or stability properties implied by the reference implementation.)
common_failure_modes: stating only feasibility; admits the zero-count no-op on amount=0 but not in general

units.description: Non-negativity: every count entry is non-negative. (Variant 5 stresses monotonicity or stability properties implied by the reference implementation.)
